Sport|Olympics

Sky Brownkicks off herOlympicscampaign atParis 2024as she competes in the women’s park skateboarding tomorrow.

The teenager won a bronze medal in Tokyo, making herTeam GB’s youngest ever medallist, and she now looks to make a return to the podium despite a recent injury scare.

Brown revealed that she suffered a dislocated shoulder the day before she travelled to Paris, admitting it was “really scary” as she feared for her participation at the Olympics.

The 16-year-old is set to have surgery when she returns home after the Games, but for now will rely on heavy strapping to minimise the pain.

Sky Brown will compete in the women’s park skateboarding heats, which are scheduled to start at 11:30am BST on Tuesday, August 6. Brown is set to go in heat four.

She will hope to book her place in the final, which will take place at 4:30pm later in the day.

TV channel:In the UK, the women’s park will be shown on BBC and Eurosport.

Live stream: Subscribers can visit the discovery+ | Paris 2024 app and website to watch the fight in full, while BBC’s coverage schedule has not yet been confirmed.

Live blog: Follow live coverage of the fight withStandard Sport’s rolling Olympics blog, with expert analysis from reporters Matt Majendie and Malik Ouzia in Paris.

title: When is Sky Brown competing at Olympics 2024? Start time and TV channel for women's park skateboarding | The Standard
author: Matt Verri
description: Brown recently dislocated her shoulder but is fit to compete in Paris
keywords: sky brown, Paris 2024, Olympics 2024, Team GB
